title: Real-world practices and barriers in endoscopic submucosal dissection training: International comprehensive survey.

signals: International survey reveals real-world ESD training gaps and barriers across multiple centers
Structured training curriculum assessment for complex endoscopic procedure
Multi-center data on skill acquisition and competency evaluation in advanced endoscopy
features.endorobotics_relevance: Identifies training bottlenecks and learning curves in manual ESD that robotic systems could address through enhanced visualization, precision, and standardized skill development platforms.

title: Water pressure method for duodenal endoscopic submucosal dissection.

signals: Novel technique for anatomically challenging lesions with improved visualization and safety
Demonstrates WPM utility beyond duodenum in high-risk anatomical sites
Submucosal layer preservation and defect closure methodology applicable to endoscopic robotics
features.endorobotics_relevance: Water pressure method offers submucosa visualization and workspace management strategy relevant to automated endoscopic dissection systems in complex anatomies.

title: A novel robotic arm-assisted endoscopic submucosal dissection platform with augmented traction for gastric neoplasms: a first-in-human prospective pilot study (with videos).

signals: First-in-human robotic ESD platform for gastric tumors with 100% en bloc resection rate
Quantified robotic performance metrics: 48.2 mm²/min dissection speed, 53.8% robot-assisted time ratio
Augmented traction mechanism directly addresses key ESD challenge of visualization and complete resection
features.endorobotics_relevance: Clinical validation of robotic arm-assisted ESD with augmented traction achieving high resection efficacy in gastric neoplasms, demonstrating core technological advantage for endoscopic robotics applications.

title: Underwater versus traction-assisted endoscopic submucosal dissection for colorectal lesions: a network meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.
affiliations: Thanyan Alghanim Gastroenterolgy and Hepatology Center

signals: Novel ESD technique variants (underwater and traction-assisted) show potential to improve conventional ESD limitations
Comparative efficacy and safety data directly relevant to endoscopic procedure optimization
R0 resection rates and procedural outcomes are critical metrics for endorobotics integration into ESD workflows
features.endorobotics_relevance: Network meta-analysis comparing innovative ESD techniques (underwater vs traction-assisted) for colorectal lesions, providing evidence base for next-generation endoscopic robotic systems that could incorporate these enhanced dissection methods.
